rawHead:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Spectral theory</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>
<!-- breadcrumb-head-auto-begin -->

rawBodyPrefix:
← Notebook
1 Spectrum 2 Self-adjoint 3 Compact 4 Bounded 5 Unbounded 6 Applications

Spectral theory

From eigenvalues of matrices to projection-valued measures: the analytic dictionary that turns operators into multiplications.

blocks.html:
1. Spectrum of a bounded operator

For a bounded operator $T$ on a Hilbert space $H$, the spectrum is $$\sigma(T) = \{\lambda \in \mathbb{C} : T-\lambda I \text{ is not invertible in } B(H)\}.$$ On finite-dimensional $H$ this is just the set of eigenvalues. In infinite dimensions $T-\lambda I$ can fail to be invertible in three different ways, and the spectrum decomposes accordingly:

  • Point spectrum $\sigma_p(T)$: $T-\lambda I$ has non-trivial kernel — $\lambda$ is a genuine eigenvalue.
  • Continuous spectrum $\sigma_c(T)$: $T-\lambda I$ is injective and its range is dense but not closed.
  • Residual spectrum $\sigma_r(T)$: $T-\lambda I$ is injective but its range is not dense.

Three theorems anchor the picture: $\sigma(T)$ is a non-empty compact subset of $\overline{B(0,\|T\|)}\subset\mathbb{C}$ (Liouville for the resolvent), and the spectral radius formula $r(T)=\lim_n \|T^n\|^{1/n}$ recovers it from the norm.

Three operators on $\ell^2(\mathbb{Z})$
Pick an operator; the spectrum lights up in the complex plane.

See also
  • Bounded operators and adjoints
  • Spectrum classification: point, continuous, residual

Used in
Spectral theory · Spectrum of self-adjoint operators
</section>

2. Self-adjoint spectrum lives on $\mathbb{R}$

If $T = T^*$ on a Hilbert space, two strong constraints follow:

  • $\sigma(T) \subset \mathbb{R}$. The proof: for $\lambda = a + ib$ with $b \ne 0$, $\|(T-\lambda)\varphi\|^2 = \|(T-a)\varphi\|^2 + b^2\|\varphi\|^2 \ge b^2\|\varphi\|^2$, so $T-\lambda$ is bounded below — and the same inequality for $T-\bar\lambda = (T-\lambda)^*$ shows the range is dense, giving invertibility.
  • $r(T) = \|T\|$. Because $\|T^2\| = \|T^*T\| = \|T\|^2$ for self-adjoint $T$, induction gives $\|T^{2^k}\| = \|T\|^{2^k}$, and the spectral-radius limit equals $\|T\|$.

The residual spectrum is also empty: if $T-\lambda$ has range not dense for $\lambda \in \mathbb{R}$, then $\overline{\mathrm{Ran}(T-\lambda)}^{\perp}=\ker(T-\lambda)^*=\ker(T-\lambda)\ne 0$, so $\lambda$ is actually an eigenvalue — contradiction. Self-adjoint spectra split into point + continuous parts only.

Random Hermitian matrix: spectrum on the real line
Resample $4\times 4$ Hermitian $A$; eigenvalues are real.

See also
  • Adjoint operator on Hilbert space

Used in
Schrödinger equation · Scattering states and barrier transmission
Spectral theory · Spectral theorem (compact self-adjoint)
</section>

3. Spectral theorem (compact self-adjoint)

The cleanest spectral statement: a compact self-adjoint operator $K$ on a separable Hilbert space admits an orthonormal basis $(\varphi_n)$ of eigenvectors with real eigenvalues $\lambda_n \to 0$. Concretely: $$K = \sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \lambda_n \langle \varphi_n, \cdot\rangle\, \varphi_n.$$ Each non-zero eigenvalue has a finite-dimensional eigenspace (compactness rules out infinite-dimensional ones). The kernel of $K$ may still be infinite-dimensional.

The canonical example: the Hilbert–Schmidt integral operator $(Kf)(x)=\int_0^1 k(x,y)f(y)\,dy$ with $k\in L^2([0,1]^2)$ symmetric ($k(x,y)=k(y,x)$). It produces a compact self-adjoint $K$, and the eigenfunction expansion is the Mercer-type series for $k$.

Eigenvalue expansion of the Green's-function kernel $k(x,y)=\min(x,y)-xy$
Take $N$ terms of the spectral series; converges uniformly to $k$.
6

See also
  • Banach and Hilbert spaces

Used in
Spectral theory · Spectral theorem (bounded self-adjoint)
</section>

4. Spectral theorem (bounded self-adjoint)

When the spectrum is no longer discrete, summation $\sum \lambda_n P_n$ must be replaced by integration. The bounded spectral theorem produces a unique projection-valued measure (PVM) $E$ on the Borel sets of $\sigma(T)\subset\mathbb{R}$ such that $$T = \int_{\sigma(T)} \lambda\, dE_\lambda, \qquad f(T) = \int_{\sigma(T)} f(\lambda)\, dE_\lambda \text{ for } f\in C(\sigma(T)).$$ $E_\Omega$ is an orthogonal projection for every Borel $\Omega$, and $E_\Omega E_{\Omega'} = E_{\Omega\cap\Omega'}$. The map $f\mapsto f(T)$ is an isometric *-homomorphism $C(\sigma(T)) \to C^*(T,1)$ — Gelfand–Naimark applied to a single self-adjoint generator.

Equivalent formulations: every bounded self-adjoint operator is unitarily equivalent to a multiplication operator $M_g\colon L^2(X,\mu)\to L^2(X,\mu)$ for some bounded measurable $g$ on a measure space $(X,\mu)$.

Projection-valued measure for $T = M_x$ on $L^2[0,1]$
Drag the interval $\Omega = [a,b]$; $E_\Omega$ is multiplication by $\mathbf{1}_\Omega$.

See also
  • Lebesgue measure
  • C*-algebras and examples

Used in
Schrödinger equation · Stationary states and the eigenvalue problem
Spectral theory · Unbounded self-adjoint operators
</section>

5. Unbounded operators and Stone's theorem

The most important operators in physics are unbounded: position $X$, momentum $P=-i\partial_x$, the Laplacian $-\Delta$, the harmonic-oscillator Hamiltonian. They cannot be elements of $B(H)$, so the bounded spectral theorem does not directly apply. The fix: work with densely defined closed operators on a domain $\mathrm{Dom}(T)\subset H$.

An operator is symmetric if $\langle T\varphi,\psi\rangle = \langle\varphi, T\psi\rangle$ for $\varphi,\psi\in\mathrm{Dom}(T)$, and self-adjoint if additionally $\mathrm{Dom}(T) = \mathrm{Dom}(T^*)$. The two conditions diverge once you leave bounded operators: symmetric is too weak. Self-adjoint operators admit a spectral resolution $T = \int_\mathbb{R} \lambda\, dE_\lambda$ on their natural domain.

Stone's theorem upgrades this to dynamics: strongly continuous one-parameter unitary groups $\{U(t)\}_{t\in\mathbb{R}}$ are in bijection with self-adjoint operators $A$ via $U(t)=e^{itA}$, with $A\varphi = -i\,\frac{d}{dt}U(t)\varphi|_{t=0}$ on the natural domain. Quantum dynamics, heat flow, and wave propagation are all instances.

Dirichlet Laplacian eigenfunctions on $[0,1]$
Compare $-u'' = \lambda u$, $u(0)=u(1)=0$ for several modes.

Used in
Spectral theory · Applications: quantum mechanics, PDE, harmonic analysis
</section>

6. Applications: quantum mechanics, PDE, harmonic analysis

Spectral theory's reach is wide:

  • Quantum mechanics. Observables are self-adjoint operators on a Hilbert space; their spectra are the possible measured values, and the spectral measure is the Born rule. Hamiltonians generate unitary time evolution $U(t)=e^{-itH/\hbar}$.
  • PDE. Elliptic operators (Laplacian, Schrödinger) on bounded domains have compact resolvent; eigenfunctions diagonalize the heat and wave semigroups. On unbounded domains, the continuous spectrum carries scattering states.
  • Harmonic analysis. Translation-invariant operators on $L^2(\mathbb{R}^n)$ are diagonalized by the Fourier transform: $\widehat{Tf}(\xi) = m(\xi)\hat f(\xi)$, the symbol $m$ being the spectral multiplier. Pontryagin duality generalizes this to all locally compact abelian groups.
Harmonic oscillator: $E_n = (n+\tfrac12)\hbar\omega$
Equally spaced energy ladder above the parabolic well.
</section>
